description_long: This object consists of four round, terracotta-colored plant pots mounted on a vertical black metal pole, arranged in a staggered pattern. The pots are evenly spaced along the pole, each supported by a circular bracket that holds them securely in place. The pole appears to have mounting hardware at both the top and bottom, suggesting it is designed to be installed between floor and ceiling or between two fixed surfaces. The pots are plain, with no visible decorative elements, and are likely made of plastic or lightweight terracotta. The structure is utilitarian and modern, suitable for maximizing vertical planting space in compact urban environments. No plants or plant labels are visible in any of the pots.
description: A vertical black metal pole supports four plain, terracotta-colored plant pots in a staggered arrangement, ideal for compact urban spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four pots clearly, staggered on both sides of the central pole.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the alignment of the pots along the pole, with only the rims of the pots visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the pole and the pots' arrangement, mirroring the front but with less detail on the pot openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the pots' rims and the vertical pole, with only two pots fully visible.
